root_text.an3.37:1.1: “Aṭṭhamiyaṁ, bhikkhave, pakkhassa catunnaṁ mahārājānaṁ amaccā pārisajjā imaṁ lokaṁ anuvicaranti:
root_text.an3.37:1.2: ‘kacci bahū manussā manussesu matteyyā petteyyā sāmaññā brahmaññā kule jeṭṭhāpacāyino uposathaṁ upavasanti paṭijāgaronti puññāni karontī’ti.
root_text.an3.37:1.3: Cātuddasiṁ, bhikkhave, pakkhassa catunnaṁ mahārājānaṁ puttā imaṁ lokaṁ anuvicaranti:
root_text.an3.37:1.4: ‘kacci bahū manussā manussesu matteyyā petteyyā sāmaññā brahmaññā kule jeṭṭhāpacāyino uposathaṁ upavasanti paṭijāgaronti puññāni karontī’ti.
root_text.an3.37:1.5: Tadahu, bhikkhave, uposathe pannarase cattāro mahārājāno sāmaññeva imaṁ lokaṁ anuvicaranti:
root_text.an3.37:1.6: ‘kacci bahū manussā manussesu matteyyā petteyyā sāmaññā brahmaññā kule jeṭṭhāpacāyino uposathaṁ upavasanti paṭijāgaronti puññāni karontī’ti.
root_text.an3.37:2.1: Sace, bhikkhave, appakā honti manussā manussesu matteyyā petteyyā sāmaññā brahmaññā kule jeṭṭhāpacāyino uposathaṁ upavasanti paṭijāgaronti puññāni karonti.
root_text.an3.37:2.2: Tamenaṁ, bhikkhave, cattāro mahārājāno devānaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ sudhammāya sabhāya sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ārocenti:
root_text.an3.37:2.3: ‘appakā kho, mārisā, manussā manussesu matteyyā petteyyā sāmaññā brahmaññā kule jeṭṭhāpacāyino uposathaṁ upavasanti paṭijāgaronti puññāni karontī’ti.
root_text.an3.37:2.4: Tena kho, bhikkhave, devā tāvatiṁsā anattamanā honti:
root_text.an3.37:2.5: ‘dibbā vata, bho, kāyā parihāyissanti, paripūrissanti asurakāyā’ti.
root_text.an3.37:3.1: Sace pana, bhikkhave, bahū honti manussā manussesu matteyyā petteyyā sāmaññā brahmaññā kule jeṭṭhāpacāyino uposathaṁ upavasanti paṭijāgaronti puññāni karonti.
root_text.an3.37:3.2: Tamenaṁ, bhikkhave, cattāro mahārājāno devānaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ sudhammāya sabhāya sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ārocenti:
root_text.an3.37:3.3: ‘bahū kho, mārisā, manussā manussesu matteyyā petteyyā sāmaññā brahmaññā kule jeṭṭhāpacāyino uposathaṁ upavasanti paṭijāgaronti puññāni karontī’ti.
root_text.an3.37:3.4: Tena, bhikkhave, devā tāvatiṁsā attamanā honti:
root_text.an3.37:3.5: ‘dibbā vata bho kāyā paripūrissanti, parihāyissanti asurakāyā’ti.
root_text.an3.37:4.1: Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, sakko devānamindo deve tāvatiṁse anunayamāno tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.an3.37:6.1: Sā kho panesā, bhikkhave, sakkena devānamindena gāthā duggītā na sugītā dubbhāsitā na subhāsitā.
root_text.an3.37:6.3: Sakko hi, bhikkhave, devānamindo avītarāgo avītadoso avītamoho.
root_text.an3.37:7.1: Yo ca kho so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu arahaṁ khīṇāsavo vusitavā brahmacariyo katakaraṇīyo ohitabhāro anuppattasadattho parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojano sammadaññāvimutto, tassa kho etaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno kallaṁ vacanāya:
root_text.an3.37:9.2: So hi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu vītarāgo vītadoso vītamoho”ti.

translation_text.an3.37:1.1: “On the eighth day of the fortnight, mendicants, the ministers and councillors of the four great kings wander about the world, thinking:
translation_text.an3.37:1.2: ‘Hopefully most humans are paying due respect to their parents, ascetics and brahmins, honoring the elders in their families, observing and keeping vigil on the sabbath, and making merit.’
translation_text.an3.37:1.3: And on the fourteenth day of the fortnight, the sons of the four great kings wander about the world, thinking:
translation_text.an3.37:1.4: ‘Hopefully most humans are paying due respect to their parents … and making merit.’
translation_text.an3.37:1.5: And on the fifteenth day sabbath, the four great kings themselves wander about the world, thinking:
translation_text.an3.37:1.6: ‘Hopefully most humans are paying due respect to their parents … and making merit.’
translation_text.an3.37:2.1: If only a few humans are paying due respect to their parents … and making merit,
translation_text.an3.37:2.2: the four great kings address the gods of the thirty-three, seated together in the Hall of Clear Right:
translation_text.an3.37:2.3: ‘Only a few humans are paying due respect to their parents … and making merit.’
translation_text.an3.37:2.4: Then the gods of the thirty-three are disappointed, thinking,
translation_text.an3.37:2.5: ‘The heavenly hosts will dwindle, while the titan hosts will swell!’
translation_text.an3.37:3.1: But if many humans are paying due respect to their parents … and making merit,
translation_text.an3.37:3.2: the four great kings address the gods of the thirty-three, seated together in the Hall of Clear Right:
translation_text.an3.37:3.3: ‘Many humans are paying due respect to their parents … and making merit.’
translation_text.an3.37:3.4: Then the gods of the thirty-three are pleased, thinking,
translation_text.an3.37:3.5: ‘The heavenly hosts will swell, while the titan hosts will dwindle!’
translation_text.an3.37:4.1: Once upon a time, Sakka, lord of gods, guiding the gods of the thirty-three, spoke this verse:
translation_text.an3.37:5.4: on the fourteenth and the fifteenth days,
translation_text.an3.37:5.5: and the eighth day of the fortnight,
translation_text.an3.37:5.6: as well as on the fortnightly special displays.’
translation_text.an3.37:6.1: But that verse was poorly sung by Sakka, lord of gods, not well sung; poorly spoken, not well spoken.
translation_text.an3.37:6.3: Sakka, lord of gods, is not free of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an3.37:7.1: But for a mendicant who is perfected—with defilements ended, who has completed the spiritual journey, done what had to be done, laid down the burden, achieved their heart’s goal, utterly ended the fetter of continued existence, and is rightly freed through enlightenment—it is appropriate to say:
translation_text.an3.37:8.4: on the fourteenth and the fifteenth days,
translation_text.an3.37:8.5: and the eighth day of the fortnight,
translation_text.an3.37:8.6: as well as on the fortnightly special displays.’
translation_text.an3.37:9.2: Because that mendicant is free of greed, hate, and delusion.”

variant_text.an3.37:2.2: mahārājāno → mahārājā (bj) | sudhammāya sabhāya → sudhammāyaṁ sabhāyaṁ (bj, sya-all, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.37:7.1: tassa kho etaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno → tassa kho etaṁ bhikkhuno (sya-all); tassa kho evaṁ bhikkhave bhikkhuno (mr)
